standards.requirement: Checkbox semantic role conveyed via native input[type=checkbox] element.
standards.implementation: Uses native <input type='checkbox'>. Role 'checkbox' is implicit.
standards.notes: Screen reader announces checkbox role natively. No additional ARIA role required.

standards.requirement: Visual elements must meet 3:1 contrast ratio against adjacent colors.
standards.implementation: Token values from design system. Formal contrast verification pending.
standards.notes: Checked state: #1B8354 (Primary) on white — needs verification. Focus ring #161616 on white — passes. Disabled: #9DA4AE border on white — may be below 3:1 (decorative/non-interactive, exempt).

standards.requirement: UI component boundaries (borders) must meet 3:1 contrast ratio against adjacent background.
standards.implementation: Default border: #6C737F on white = ~4.5:1 — expected to pass. Formal verification pending.
standards.notes: Disabled border exempt from non-text contrast requirements as component is non-interactive.

standards.implementation: Native <input type='checkbox'> provides Space key toggle natively. No custom JS keyboard handling required.
standards.notes: Tab navigation and Space activation handled by browser. Read-only uses pointer-events:none + aria-readonly — remains in tab order.

standards.requirement: Keyboard focus indicator must be visible.
standards.implementation: Focus ring via ::after pseudo on .checkbox__box, triggered by :focus-visible. 2px solid #161616 border. Appears on keyboard focus, suppressed on mouse click.
standards.notes: mix-blend-mode: multiply applied to match Figma design intent. Focus ring size: box + 8px offset.

standards.requirement: Focus indicator must have minimum 2px width and sufficient contrast.
standards.implementation: Focus ring: 2px solid var(--checkbox-focus-ring-color, #161616). Enclosure area exceeds box by 8px.
standards.notes: Formal verification of contrast and enclosure area pending external audit.

standards.requirement: UI components must have accessible name, role, and current state.
standards.implementation: Role: implicit 'checkbox' via input[type=checkbox]. State: :checked maps to aria-checked=true. Indeterminate: requires JS to set aria-checked='mixed'. Read-only: requires aria-readonly=true. Disabled: disabled attribute maps to aria-disabled=true.
standards.notes: Accessible name (Name) is NOT provided by this component. Consumer or form-field assembly must provide aria-label or associated label element.

standards.criterion: Control States — Required states for interactive controls
standards.requirement: Interactive controls must implement: default, hover, focus, active/pressed, disabled.
standards.implementation: Default, Hovered, Pressed, Focused, Disabled implemented. Read-only also implemented.
standards.notes: Error state not implemented at component level. Form-field is responsible for error state visual treatment.

standards.requirement: Checkbox must support indeterminate (mixed) state for hierarchical selection patterns.
standards.implementation: CSS :indeterminate pseudo-class with indeterminate mark SVG. JS must set input.indeterminate = true and aria-checked='mixed'.
standards.notes: Template.html demonstrates the HTML structure. JS wiring not included — consumer responsibility.

standards.requirement: Component CSS must reference component-level tokens (--checkbox-*), not raw primitives.
standards.implementation: Component CSS defines --checkbox-* tokens as wrappers over Controls-* and Global-* tokens. No hex values or primitives used directly in component selectors.
standards.notes: --checkbox-* tokens not yet defined in token.css. CSS uses var(--checkbox-*, fallback) pattern. Requires token.css update.

standards.requirement: Components must render correctly in Arabic (RTL) layouts.
standards.implementation: Component is geometrically symmetric. No directional CSS properties used. No RTL-specific overrides required.
standards.notes: Verified: no left/right, padding-left/right, margin-left/right, border-left/right properties that would break RTL. Uses flexbox centering.

standards.requirement: Touch targets should be at least 44×44 CSS pixels.
standards.implementation: Component box sizes: 16px, 20px, 24px. Ripple provides visual affordance but not structural hit target. Consumer must ensure adequate label or wrapper size.
standards.notes: AAA level — advisory only. Form-field wrapper is responsible for ensuring adequate touch target.
